title: Wash Yuh Hands Clean!
description: A fun soca song teaching kids about the importance of washing their hands to stay healthy.
lyrics:
[Chorus]
Wash yuh hands, wash yuh hands, [CLAP] wash them clean!
With soap and water, sparkling sheen!
Wash yuh hands, wash yuh hands, [CLAP] every day,
To keep the germs and sickness away!

[Verse 1]
After playing in the sun so bright,
Wash yuh hands before yuh bite!
Dirt and sand, dey love to hide,
So wash them off with soapy tide!

[Verse 2]
After using the toilet bowl,
Wash yuh hands to make yuh whole!
Germs dey sneaky, germs dey small,
Wash them off, one and all!

[Verse 3]
Before you eat yuh roti nice,
Wash yuh hands, follow my advice!
Mango juice and bake so sweet,
But clean yuh hands before you eat!

[Chorus]
Wash yuh hands, wash yuh hands, [CLAP] wash them clean!
With soap and water, sparkling sheen!
Wash yuh hands, wash yuh hands, [CLAP] every day,
To keep the germs and sickness away!

[Outro]
'Yes sah! Doh be lazy, wash yuh hands good!' (Yes sir! Don't be lazy, wash your hands well!)
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant and colorful illustration of children dancing to soca music in a tropical Caribbean setting with palm trees, a steelpan drum, and clear blue ocean in the background. The children are actively washing their hands at an outdoor sink with soap bubbles floating around them. The sun is shining brightly, and the overall mood is cheerful and energetic. The title "Wash Yuh Hands Clean!" is prominently displayed in a fun, cartoonish font. One child holds a roti.
educationalValue: Teaches children the importance of handwashing to prevent the spread of germs and stay healthy. Introduces simple Patois and Caribbean cultural references.
